mission.title: helm: `helm upgrade --install` doesn't perform an install/upgrade if the first ever install fails
mission.description: `helm upgrade --install` doesn't perform an install/upgrade if the first ever install fails. This issue affects 87+ users.

steps.title: Identify helm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your helm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n helm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `UPGRADE FAILED: "foo" has no deployed releases`

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get configmap -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=helm
```
Using `helm upgrade --install` is a nice way to install or upgrade depending on if the release exists. But it looks like there's a bug in the logic; its not handling failed installs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for `helm upgrade --install` doesn't perform an install/upgrade
steps.description:
If the first `upgrade --install` results in a state FAILED, you can not
run the same command `upgrade --install` again without a failure.
This happens becuase we are search only for releases with the status DEPLOYED.
This change will if the search for DEPLOYED fails, then try to search for a
```yaml
$ helm list
NAME           	REVISION	UPDATED                 	STATUS  	CHART                         	NAMESPACE
foo            	2       	Wed Jan 17 11:48:08 2018	FAILED	something-0.0.1                     	default

$ helm upgrade "foo" . --install 
Error: UPGRADE FAILED: "foo" has no deployed releases
```

steps.title: Upgrade helm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade helm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade helm helm/helm --namespace helm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm
helm list -n helm
```

steps.title: Confirm `helm upgrade --install` doesn't perform an is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm -n helm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n helm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `UPGRADE FAILED: "foo" has no deployed releases` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
If the first `upgrade --install` results in a state FAILED, you can not
run the same command `upgrade --install` again without a failure.
This happens becuase we are search only for releases with the status DEPLOYED.
This change will if the search for DEPLOYED fails, then try to search for a release
with the state FAILED, and if found upgrade that.

This fixes issue #3353
codeSnippets:
$ helm list
NAME           	REVISION	UPDATED                 	STATUS  	CHART                         	NAMESPACE
foo            	2       	Wed Jan 17 11:48:08 2018	FAILED	something-0.0.1                     	default

$ helm upgrade "foo" . --install 
Error: UPGRADE FAILED: "foo" has no deployed releases

History prints historical revisions for a given release.

A default maximum of 256 revisions will be returned. Setting '--max'
configures the maximum length of the revision list returned.

The historical release set is printed as a formatted table, e.g:

    $ helm history angry-bird --max=4
    REVISION   UPDATED                      STATUS           CHART        DESCRIPTION
    1           Mon Oct 3 10:15:13 2016     SUPERSEDED      alpine-0.1.0  Initial install
    2           Mon Oct 3 10:15:13 2016     SUPERSEDED      alpine-0.1.0  Upgraded successfully
    3           Mon Oct 3 10:15:13 2016     SUPERSEDED      alpine-0.1.0  Rolled back to 2
    4           Mon Oct 3 10:15:13 2016     DEPLOYED        alpine-0.1.0  Upgraded successfully

if helm history --max 1 "$name" 2>/dev/null | grep FAILED | cut -f1 | grep -q 1; then
    helm delete --purge "$name"
fi

helm upgrade --install --wait "$release" chart/

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
